parametrized_description: An oil and gas company sends oil to the port using containers and trucks. Each container can hold G units of oil while each truck can hold Y units of oil. Due to government restrictions, the number of trucks used must be at most K multiplied by the number of containers used. If at least V units of oil need to be sent to the port and at least L containers must be used, minimize the total number of containers and trucks needed.

L.description: The fewest amount of containers required

V.description: The smallest quantity of oil units required to be transported to the port.

G.description: The quantity of oil that each container is capable of holding.

Y.description: Quantity of oil that each truck is capable of carrying

K.description: The highest permitted proportion of trucks to containers

constraints.description: The maximum amount of trucks allowed is half the number of containers used.
A minimum of 15 containers must be utilized.
A minimum of 2000 units of oil must be delivered to the port, with the calculation being 30 units per container and 40 units per truck.

objective.description: The goal is to reduce the overall requirement for containers and trucks to a minimum.
